feat(tools): add JFP tool support and expand CLI

New tooling and CLI enhancements:

internal/tools/jfp.go (+28 lines):
- Add JFP (JSON File Processor) tool implementation
- Integrate with existing tool infrastructure

internal/tools/contract_test.go (+45 lines):
- Add contract tests for new tool

testdata/faketools/jfp (+24 lines):
- Add fake JFP tool for testing

internal/cli/root.go (+37 lines):
- Expand CLI with new command options
- Improve command routing

docs/robot-api-design.md:
- Documentation updates

Also sync beads issue tracker state.

 {"id":"bd-1mcvs","title":"Task: Add rate_limit_detected field with pattern matching","description":"## What

Add `rate_limit_detected` boolean field that pattern-matches pane output
for rate limit indicators.

## Why

Rate limiting is the #1 failure mode in multi-agent sessions. Operators
currently must manually grep pane output to detect this. Automated detection
enables automated response (wait, switch account, skip pane).

## Rate Limit Patterns

Known patterns to detect:

| Provider | Pattern |
|----------|---------|
| Claude/Anthropic | "You've hit your limit" |
| Claude/Anthropic | "resets .* (America/.*)" |
| OpenAI | "Rate limit exceeded" |
| OpenAI | "Too many requests" |
| Google | "RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED" |
| Generic | "rate limit" (case insensitive) |

## Implementation

```go
var rateLimitPatterns = []*regexp.Regexp{
    regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)you've hit your limit`),
    regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)rate limit`),
    regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)too many requests`),
    regexp.MustCompile(`RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED`),
    regexp.MustCompile(`resets \d+[ap]m`),
}

func detectRateLimit(paneContent string) (bool, string) {
    // Check last 50 lines only (performance)
    lines := strings.Split(paneContent, "\n")
    if len(lines) > 50 {
        lines = lines[len(lines)-50:]
    }
    content := strings.Join(lines, "\n")
    
    for _, pattern := range rateLimitPatterns {
        if match := pattern.FindString(content); match != "" {
            return true, match
        }
    }
    return false, ""
}
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "panes": [{
    "index": 4,
    "rate_limit_detected": true,
    "rate_limit_match": "You've hit your limit",
    "rate_limit_reset": "4pm ET"  // Parsed if available
  }]
}
```

## Performance

- Only check last 50 lines (configurable)
- Compile patterns once at startup
- Short-circuit on first match

## Configuration

Allow custom patterns via config for unknown providers:
```yaml
rate_limit_patterns:
  - "custom rate limit message"
```

## Testing

- Unit test: Each pattern with mock content
- Integration test: Actual rate-limited pane","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:18:44.314512352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:56:06.806832590Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:56:06.806782305Z","close_reason":"Task already implemented.  {"id":"bd-1mdym","title":"Task: Implement --robot-ensemble-modes and --robot-ensemble-presets","description":"# Task: Implement --robot-ensemble-modes and --robot-ensemble-presets

Implement robot commands for listing available modes and ensembles.

## Command Specification
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-modes [--category CATEGORY] [--tier core|advanced|experimental|all] [--limit N] [--offset N]
ntm --robot-ensemble-presets
```

- Modifiers are **unprefixed** per robot-api-design.md (`--category`, `--tier`, `--limit`, `--offset`).
- `--category` accepts code (A-L) or name (Formal, Ampliative, ...).

## Output Schema

### Full Mode List (default = core tier only)
```json
{
  "action": "ensemble_modes",
  "modes": [
    {
      "id": "deductive",
      "code": "A1",
      "tier": "core",
      "name": "Deductive Inference",
      "category": {"code": "A", "name": "Formal"},
      "short_desc": "Classical logic reasoning",
      "description": "...",
      "outputs": "Valid/invalid argument classification",
      "best_for": ["Contract validation"],
      "differentiator": "Unlike induction (B1)...",
      "failure_modes": ["Hidden premises"],
      "icon": "⊢",
      "color": "#A1A1A1"
    }
  ],
  "categories": [
    {"code": "A", "name": "Formal", "mode_count": 8}
  ],
  "default_tier": "core",
  "total_modes": 80,
  "core_modes": 28,
  "advanced_modes": 52,
  "experimental_modes": 0
}
```

### Category + Tier Filter
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-modes --category Formal --tier advanced
```

### Presets List
```json
{
  "action": "ensemble_presets",
  "presets": [
    {
      "name": "project-diagnosis",
      "display": "Project Diagnosis",
      "description": "Comprehensive project health analysis",
      "modes": ["systems-thinking", "failure-mode", "perspective-taking"],
      "synthesis": {"strategy": "adversarial"},
      "budget": {"total": 30000},
      "allow_advanced": false
    }
  ]
}
```

## Implementation
```go
// internal/robot/ensemble_modes.go
func HandleEnsembleModes(category, tier string, limit, offset int) (*EnsembleModesResponse, error)
func filterByCategory(modes []Mode, category string) []Mode
func filterByTier(modes []Mode, tier string) []Mode
func buildCategorySummary(modes []Mode) []CategoryInfo
```

### File Location
internal/robot/ensemble_modes.go
internal/robot/ensemble_presets.go

### Dependencies
- Requires mode catalog (bd-qn9gc)
- Tier metadata support (bd-3806s)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default output only includes core tier
- [ ] Tier filter works (core/advanced/experimental/all)
- [ ] Category filter accepts code or name
- [ ] JSON includes tier + counts (incl. experimental)
- [ ] RobotResponse envelope compliance
- [ ] Arrays initialized to [] when empty","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:40:59.724643849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:50:29.572177836Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:50:29.572100713Z","close_reason":"Implemented robot API for ensemble modes (--robot-ensemble-modes) and presets (--robot-ensemble-presets) with full test coverage","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mdym","depends_on_id":"bd-1ovi8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mdym","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mdym","depends_on_id":"bd-qn9gc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1pt6v","title":"Implement --pane flag for single-pane diagnosis","description":"Implement --pane flag for single-pane diagnosis.

## What

Add --pane=N flag to diagnose a specific pane instead of all panes.

## Use Cases

- Quick check on suspected problem pane
- Debugging a specific agent
- Reduced output when you know where the issue is

## Implementation

```go
func diagnose(session string, paneFilter *int) DiagnoseResult {
    panes := getAllPanes(session)
    
    if paneFilter != nil {
        // Filter to single pane
        panes = filterToPanes(panes, []int{*paneFilter})
        if len(panes) == 0 {
            return errorResult("PANE_NOT_FOUND", paneFilter)
        }
    }
    
    // Continue with diagnosis on filtered panes
    ...
}
```

With --pane=4:

```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "filter": {"pane": 4},
  "panes": {
    "healthy": [],
    "rate_limited": [4]
  },
  "recommendations": [{...single recommendation for pane 4...}]
}
```

 {"id":"bd-1q93","title":"Fix progress bar rendering artifacts in context usage display","description":"## Problem
Context usage progress bar showing visual artifacts instead of clean bar.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Implementation Files:
- `internal/tui/components/progress.go:73-118` - ProgressBar.View()
- `internal/tui/styles/styles.go:199-252` - Shimmer() function
- `internal/tui/styles/styles.go:317-347` - ShimmerProgressBar()
- `internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go:2606-2640` - renderContextBar()

### Characters Used:
- Filled: `█` (U+2588 Full Block)
- Empty: `░` (U+2591 Light Shade)
- Alternative: `━` `┄` in metrics panel

### How Shimmer Works:
```go
// Each character gets individual RGB color code
for i, r := range runes {
    pos := float64(i)/float64(n-1) + offset  // offset changes with tick
    // Calculate interpolated color
    color := Lerp(parsedColors[segmentIdx], parsedColors[segmentIdx+1], localPos)
    style := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color(fmt.Sprintf("#%02x%02x%02x", c.R, c.G, c.B)))
    result.WriteString(style.Render(string(r)))
}
```

### Potential Artifact Causes:
1. **True Color Fallback** - Terminal may not support 24-bit RGB, causing color dithering
2. **Per-character ANSI** - Each block has its own escape sequence (e.g., `\x1b[38;2;100;150;200m█\x1b[0m`)
3. **Font Glyph Width** - `█` and `░` may render at different widths in some fonts
4. **Animation Jitter** - Shimmer updates every 50ms causing visual noise
5. **Unicode Rendering** - Block characters may have combining issues

## Fix Approach

### 1. Add Terminal Capability Detection
```go
// internal/tui/terminal/caps.go
func SupportsTrueColor() bool {
    colorterm := os.Getenv("COLORTERM")
    return colorterm == "truecolor" || colorterm == "24bit"
}

func SupportsUnicodeBlocks() bool {
    // Check TERM and LC_CTYPE for Unicode support
    term := os.Getenv("TERM")
    return strings.Contains(term, "256color") || strings.Contains(term, "xterm")
}
```

### 2. Provide ASCII Fallback
```go
// Fallback characters for limited terminals
const (
    FilledASCII = "="
    EmptyASCII  = "-"
    FilledSimple = "#"
    EmptySimple  = "."
)

func (p ProgressBar) View() string {
    filled, empty := p.FilledChar, p.EmptyChar
    if !terminal.SupportsUnicodeBlocks() {
        filled, empty = FilledASCII, EmptyASCII
    }
    // ... rest of render
}
```

### 3. Reduce Shimmer Frequency
```go
// Change from 50ms to 100-200ms to reduce visual noise
func (p ProgressBar) tick() tea.Cmd {
    return tea.Tick(time.Millisecond*150, func(t time.Time) tea.Msg {
        return ProgressTickMsg(t)
    })
}
```

### 4. Simplify Color Gradient
```go
// Use solid color instead of per-character gradient for better stability
func SimpleProgressBar(percent float64, width int, filled, empty string, filledColor, emptyColor lipgloss.Color) string {
    filledWidth := int(percent * float64(width))
    emptyWidth := width - filledWidth
    
    filledStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(filledColor)
    emptyStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(emptyColor)
    
    return filledStyle.Render(strings.Repeat(filled, filledWidth)) +
           emptyStyle.Render(strings.Repeat(empty, emptyWidth))
}
```

## Unit Tests Required
```go
func TestProgressBarRendering(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name    string
        percent float64
        width   int
    }{
        {"empty", 0.0, 20},
        {"half", 0.5, 20},
        {"full", 1.0, 20},
        {"narrow", 0.5, 5},
        {"overflow", 1.5, 20},  // should clamp to 1.0
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            bar := NewProgressBar(tc.width)
            bar.SetPercent(tc.percent)
            bar.Animated = false  // Disable for deterministic test
            
            rendered := bar.View()
            
            // Verify display width matches expected
            expectedWidth := tc.width
            if bar.ShowPercent {
                expectedWidth += 5  // " 100%"
            }
            
            actualWidth := lipgloss.Width(rendered)
            if actualWidth != expectedWidth {
                t.Errorf("width = %d, want %d", actualWidth, expectedWidth)
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestShimmerStability(t *testing.T) {
    // Verify shimmer produces stable width across ticks
    text := strings.Repeat("█", 10)
    colors := []string{"#FF0000", "#00FF00", "#0000FF"}
    
    var widths []int
    for tick := 0; tick < 100; tick++ {
        result := styles.Shimmer(text, tick, colors...)
        widths = append(widths, lipgloss.Width(result))
    }
    
    // All widths should be identical (10)
    for i, w := range widths {
        if w != 10 {
            t.Errorf("tick %d: width = %d, want 10", i, w)
        }
    }
}

func TestASCIIFallback(t *testing.T) {
    bar := NewProgressBar(20)
    bar.SetPercent(0.5)
    bar.FilledChar = "="
    bar.EmptyChar = "-"
    bar.Animated = false
    
    rendered := bar.View()
    
    // Verify no Unicode block characters
    if strings.ContainsAny(rendered, "█░") {
        t.Error("ASCII fallback should not contain Unicode blocks")
    }
    
    // Verify correct character count
    stripped := stripANSI(rendered)
    equals := strings.Count(stripped, "=")
    dashes := strings.Count(stripped, "-")
    
    if equals != 10 || dashes != 10 {
        t.Errorf("got %d equals, %d dashes; want 10 each", equals, dashes)
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-5dfye","title":"Task: Redaction spec + pattern catalog","description":"## Goal
Define the canonical set of detection categories + patterns and how they map to user-facing messages and redaction placeholders.

**Explicit stance (v1):** prioritize *secrets*; defer PII (email/phone/SSN/credit-card) behind a future opt-in or stricter safety profile.

## Source of Truth (current implementation)
- Engine: `internal/safety/redaction` (modes: off|warn|redact|block, allowlist support)
- Canonical fixture corpus: `testdata/redaction_fixtures.json` (loaded by `internal/safety/redaction/redaction_test.go`)
- Legacy regex list to migrate away from: `internal/checkpoint/export.go` (`secretPatterns`)

## Canonical Categories (v1)
Use these exact tokens (matches `internal/safety/redaction.Category`):
- `OPENAI_KEY`
- `ANTHROPIC_KEY`
- `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- `GOOGLE_API_KEY`
- `AWS_ACCESS_KEY`
- `AWS_SECRET_KEY`
- `JWT`
- `BEARER_TOKEN`
- `PRIVATE_KEY`
- `DATABASE_URL`
- `PASSWORD`
- `GENERIC_API_KEY`
- `GENERIC_SECRET`

## Pattern Catalog (v1)
The authoritative list is `internal/safety/redaction/patterns.go` (ordered by priority; higher = more specific).

**Provider keys (high priority)**
- `OPENAI_KEY`
  - `sk-...T3BlbkFJ...` (OpenAI base64 marker)
  - `sk-proj-...` (project keys)
- `ANTHROPIC_KEY`
  - `sk-ant-...`
- `GITHUB_TOKEN`
  - `gh[pousr]_...` (classic/oauth/app/user tokens)
  - `github_pat_..._...` (fine-grained PAT)
- `GOOGLE_API_KEY`
  - `AIza...` (Google API key)

**Cloud credentials**
- `AWS_ACCESS_KEY`
  - `AKIA...` (long-lived)
  - `ASIA...` (STS)
- `AWS_SECRET_KEY`
  - assignment forms containing `aws_secret` / `secret_access_key` / `secret_key`

**Auth tokens**
- `JWT`
  - `eyJ... . eyJ... . ...`
- `BEARER_TOKEN`
  - `bearer <token>` (header or inline)

**Other high-signal secrets**
- `PRIVATE_KEY`
  - `-----BEGIN ... PRIVATE KEY-----`
- `DATABASE_URL`
  - `postgres|mysql|mongodb|redis://user:pass@host...`

**Generic key/value secrets (lower priority)**
- `PASSWORD`
  - `password|passwd|pwd = ...`
- `GENERIC_API_KEY`
  - `*_api_key|apikey = ...`
- `GENERIC_SECRET`
  - `secret|private_key|token = ...`

### Overlap / precedence strategy
- Prefer provider-specific matches over generic matches.
- If overlaps exist, keep the higher-priority match and drop the lower-priority one.
- This behavior is tested in `internal/safety/redaction/redaction_test.go` (overlap + order invariants).

## Redaction Placeholder Strategy
- Placeholder format: `[REDACTED:{CATEGORY}:{hash8}]`
- `hash8` = first 8 lowercase hex chars of `SHA-256("{CATEGORY}:{MATCH}")`
- Hash is deterministic + fixed-length (does not leak the match length).

## UX / Messaging
- `warn`: surface category + where (prompt vs output vs export) + how to override (allowlist / disable category / per-command flag).
- `block`: fail the operation with a clear `error_code` (robot) and guidance.

## False-Positive Strategy + Allowlist Escape Hatch
- Keep generic patterns low priority.
- Allowlist is regex-based and must suppress findings **by match**, not by category.

## Inventory: legacy checkpoint-export patterns → categories
`internal/checkpoint/export.go` currently redacts by replacing matches with `[REDACTED]`. These should be mapped onto the categories above and routed through the shared engine:
- `(?i)(api[_-]?key|apikey) ...` → `GENERIC_API_KEY`
- `(?i)(secret|password|passwd|pwd) ...` → `PASSWORD` (and/or `GENERIC_SECRET` for non-password tokens)
- `(?i)(token|bearer) ...` / `Authorization: Bearer ...` → `BEARER_TOKEN`
- `AKIA...` → `AWS_ACCESS_KEY`
- `aws_secret|aws_access ...` → `AWS_SECRET_KEY` / `AWS_ACCESS_KEY` (split)
- `ghp_...` → `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- `sk-...` → `OPENAI_KEY`
- `sk-ant-...` → `ANTHROPIC_KEY`
- `-----BEGIN ... PRIVATE KEY-----` → `PRIVATE_KEY`

## Fixtures
- `testdata/redaction_fixtures.json` contains **≥30 true-positive fixtures across categories**, plus negatives + edge cases.
- The file uses the `<<OPENAI_TEST_KEY>>` placeholder to avoid triggering secret scanners; tests substitute a synthetic key.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonFox","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:20:29.318977840Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:38:25.766543229Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:35:41.927121348Z","close_reason":"Completed: canonical spec + fixture catalog in docs/REDACTION_SPEC.md (includes checkpoint export regex regression set)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5dfye","depends_on_id":"bd-3cgwv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Implement the handoff writer module that serializes Handoff structs to YAML files in the correct directory structure with atomic writes and comprehensive logging.

## Background & Reasoning
Handoffs must be stored in a predictable location so that:
1. Session-start can find the most recent handoff
2. Status line can extract goal/now fields
3. Multiple sessions can coexist without conflicts
4. Files are human-readable for debugging
5. Concurrent writes don't corrupt data

## Directory Structure

```
.ntm/
├── handoffs/
│   ├── {session-name}/
│   │   ├── 2026-01-19_14-30_auth-complete.yaml
│   │   ├── 2026-01-19_16-00_tests-passing.yaml
│   │   ├── auto-handoff-2026-01-19T14-30-00.yaml
│   │   └── .archive/  # Old handoffs moved here after rotation
│   │       └── 2026-01-18_10-00_old-handoff.yaml
│   └── general/
└── ledgers/
```

### 1. Create internal/handoff/writer.go
```go
package handoff

import (
    "fmt"
    "log/slog"
    "os"
    "path/filepath"
    "regexp"
    "strings"
    "sync"
    "time"

    "gopkg.in/yaml.v3"
)

var (
    // Mutex for concurrent write protection
    writeMu sync.Mutex

    // Valid session name pattern
    sessionNameRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`)

    // Valid description pattern (kebab-case)
    descriptionRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-z0-9-]+$`)
)

// Writer handles writing handoff files to disk
type Writer struct {
    baseDir    string // typically .ntm/handoffs
    maxPerDir  int    // max handoffs before rotation (default 50)
    logger     *slog.Logger
}

// NewWriter creates a Writer for the given project directory
func NewWriter(projectDir string) *Writer {
    return &Writer{
        baseDir:   filepath.Join(projectDir, ".ntm", "handoffs"),
        maxPerDir: 50,
        logger:    slog.Default().With("component", "handoff.writer"),
    }
}

// Write saves a handoff to the appropriate directory using atomic write
// Returns the path to the written file
func (w *Writer) Write(h *Handoff, description string) (string, error) {
    writeMu.Lock()
    defer writeMu.Unlock()

    w.logger.Debug("starting handoff write",
        "session", h.Session,
        "description", description,
        "goal_length", len(h.Goal),
        "now_length", len(h.Now),
    )

    // Validate handoff
    if errs := h.Validate(); len(errs) > 0 {
        w.logger.Error("handoff validation failed",
            "session", h.Session,
            "error_count", len(errs),
            "first_error", errs[0].Error(),
        )
        return "", fmt.Errorf("validation failed: %v", errs[0])
    }

    // Validate session name
    if h.Session != "general" && !sessionNameRegex.MatchString(h.Session) {
        w.logger.Error("invalid session name",
            "session", h.Session,
        )
        return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid session name: %s", h.Session)
    }

    // Sanitize description
    desc := sanitizeDescription(description)
    if desc == "" {
        desc = "handoff"
    }

    // Ensure directory exists
    if err := w.EnsureDir(h.Session); err != nil {
        return "", err
    }

    // Check for rotation
    if err := w.checkRotation(h.Session); err != nil {
        w.logger.Warn("rotation check failed", "error", err)
        // Non-fatal, continue with write
    }

    // Set defaults
    h.SetDefaults()

    // Generate filename
    filename := fmt.Sprintf("%s_%s.yaml",
        time.Now().Format("2006-01-02_15-04"),
        desc,
    )
    path := filepath.Join(w.baseDir, h.Session, filename)

    // Serialize to YAML
    data, err := yaml.Marshal(h)
    if err != nil {
        w.logger.Error("yaml marshaling failed",
            "session", h.Session,
            "error", err,
        )
        return "", fmt.Errorf("marshal failed: %w", err)
    }

    // Atomic write: write to temp, then rename
    if err := w.atomicWrite(path, data); err != nil {
        return "", err
    }

    w.logger.Info("handoff written successfully",
        "path", path,
        "session", h.Session,
        "goal", truncateLog(h.Goal, 50),
        "now", truncateLog(h.Now, 50),
        "size_bytes", len(data),
    )

    return path, nil
}

// WriteAuto saves an auto-generated handoff with timestamp naming
func (w *Writer) WriteAuto(h *Handoff) (string, error) {
    writeMu.Lock()
    defer writeMu.Unlock()

    w.logger.Debug("starting auto-handoff write",
        "session", h.Session,
        "tokens_pct", h.TokensPct,
    )

    // Validate
    if errs := h.Validate(); len(errs) > 0 {
        w.logger.Error("auto-handoff validation failed",
            "session", h.Session,
            "error_count", len(errs),
        )
        return "", fmt.Errorf("validation failed: %v", errs[0])
    }

    // Ensure directory
    if err := w.EnsureDir(h.Session); err != nil {
        return "", err
    }

    // Set defaults
    h.SetDefaults()

    // Generate auto filename with ISO8601 timestamp
    filename := fmt.Sprintf("auto-handoff-%s.yaml",
        time.Now().Format("2006-01-02T15-04-05"),
    )
    path := filepath.Join(w.baseDir, h.Session, filename)

    // Serialize
    data, err := yaml.Marshal(h)
    if err != nil {
        return "", fmt.Errorf("marshal failed: %w", err)
    }

    // Atomic write
    if err := w.atomicWrite(path, data); err != nil {
        return "", err
    }

    w.logger.Info("auto-handoff written",
        "path", path,
        "session", h.Session,
        "tokens_pct", h.TokensPct,
        "size_bytes", len(data),
    )

    return path, nil
}

// EnsureDir creates the handoff directory for a session if needed
func (w *Writer) EnsureDir(sessionName string) error {
    dir := filepath.Join(w.baseDir, sessionName)

    if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); err != nil {
        w.logger.Error("failed to create handoff directory",
            "dir", dir,
            "error", err,
        )
        return fmt.Errorf("mkdir failed: %w", err)
    }

    w.logger.Debug("ensured handoff directory", "dir", dir)
    return nil
}

// atomicWrite writes data to a temp file then renames to target
func (w *Writer) atomicWrite(path string, data []byte) error {
    dir := filepath.Dir(path)

    // Create temp file in same directory (ensures same filesystem)
    tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(dir, ".handoff-*.tmp")
    if err != nil {
        w.logger.Error("failed to create temp file",
            "dir", dir,
            "error", err,
        )
        return fmt.Errorf("temp file creation failed: %w", err)
    }
    tmpPath := tmp.Name()

    // Cleanup on failure
    defer func() {
        if tmpPath != "" {
            os.Remove(tmpPath)
        }
    }()

    // Write data
    if _, err := tmp.Write(data); err != nil {
        tmp.Close()
        w.logger.Error("failed to write temp file",
            "path", tmpPath,
            "error", err,
        )
        return fmt.Errorf("write failed: %w", err)
    }

    // Sync to disk
    if err := tmp.Sync(); err != nil {
        tmp.Close()
        w.logger.Error("failed to sync temp file",
            "path", tmpPath,
            "error", err,
        )
        return fmt.Errorf("sync failed: %w", err)
    }

    // Close before rename
    if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("close failed: %w", err)
    }

    // Set permissions
    if err := os.Chmod(tmpPath, 0644); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("chmod failed: %w", err)
    }

    // Atomic rename
    if err := os.Rename(tmpPath, path); err != nil {
        w.logger.Error("failed to rename temp to target",
            "temp", tmpPath,
            "target", path,
            "error", err,
        )
        return fmt.Errorf("rename failed: %w", err)
    }

    tmpPath = "" // Prevent cleanup of successful write
    return nil
}

// checkRotation moves old handoffs to .archive if count exceeds max
func (w *Writer) checkRotation(sessionName string) error {
    dir := filepath.Join(w.baseDir, sessionName)
    archiveDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".archive")

    entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }

    // Count YAML files (exclude .archive dir)
    var yamlFiles []os.DirEntry
    for _, e := range entries {
        if !e.IsDir() && strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), ".yaml") {
            yamlFiles = append(yamlFiles, e)
        }
    }

    if len(yamlFiles) <= w.maxPerDir {
        return nil // No rotation needed
    }

    // Create archive dir
    if err := os.MkdirAll(archiveDir, 0755); err != nil {
        return err
    }

    // Sort by name (which includes timestamp) - oldest first
    // Move oldest files to archive
    toMove := len(yamlFiles) - w.maxPerDir
    for i := 0; i < toMove; i++ {
        oldPath := filepath.Join(dir, yamlFiles[i].Name())
        newPath := filepath.Join(archiveDir, yamlFiles[i].Name())
        if err := os.Rename(oldPath, newPath); err != nil {
            w.logger.Warn("failed to archive handoff",
                "path", oldPath,
                "error", err,
            )
        } else {
            w.logger.Debug("archived old handoff",
                "from", oldPath,
                "to", newPath,
            )
        }
    }

    w.logger.Info("handoff rotation completed",
        "session", sessionName,
        "archived", toMove,
    )

    return nil
}

// sanitizeDescription converts to kebab-case and limits length
func sanitizeDescription(desc string) string {
    // Lowercase
    desc = strings.ToLower(desc)
    // Replace spaces/underscores with hyphens
    desc = strings.ReplaceAll(desc, " ", "-")
    desc = strings.ReplaceAll(desc, "_", "-")
    // Remove non-alphanumeric except hyphens
    var result strings.Builder
    for _, r := range desc {
        if (r >= 'a' && r <= 'z') || (r >= '0' && r <= '9') || r == '-' {
            result.WriteRune(r)
        }
    }
    desc = result.String()
    // Collapse multiple hyphens
    for strings.Contains(desc, "--") {
        desc = strings.ReplaceAll(desc, "--", "-")
    }
    // Trim hyphens from ends
    desc = strings.Trim(desc, "-")
    // Limit length
    if len(desc) > 50 {
        desc = desc[:50]
    }
    return desc
}

// truncateLog truncates string for logging
func truncateLog(s string, max int) string {
    if len(s) <= max {
        return s
    }
    return s[:max] + "..."
}
```

### 2. File Naming Convention
- Manual: `YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM_{description}.yaml`
- Auto: `auto-handoff-{ISO8601-timestamp}.yaml`
- Description: kebab-case, max 50 chars, sanitized

```yaml
session: myproject
```

### 4. Rotation Policy
- Default: Keep 50 most recent handoffs per session
- Older handoffs moved to `.archive/` subdirectory
- Archive provides audit trail without cluttering main directory
- Configurable via Writer.maxPerDir

 {"id":"bd-k3zq","title":"Add ntm assign command - combines bv triage with ntm send in one step","description":"## Overview
Canonical work distribution command: `ntm assign`. Combines BV triage with ntm send.

## Supported Strategies
- **balanced** (default): Balance workload across idle agents, consider current assignment counts
- **speed**: Prioritize agents with low context usage for fast completion
- **quality**: Match agent type to bead type using capability matrix
- **dependency**: Prioritize beads that unblock the most downstream work (high PageRank/betweenness)
- **round-robin**: Deterministic even distribution (bead[i] -> agent[i % N])

## Command Design
```bash
# Preview assignments (default - no sending)
ntm assign myproject

# Execute assignments immediately
ntm assign myproject --auto

# With strategy
ntm assign myproject --strategy=quality --auto

# Limit number of assignments
ntm assign myproject --limit=5 --auto

# Assign specific beads
ntm assign myproject --beads=bd-123,bd-456 --auto

# Filter agent types (two naming styles for convenience)
ntm assign myproject --cc-only --auto
ntm assign myproject --agent=claude --auto    # Equivalent
ntm assign myproject --agent=codex --auto
ntm assign myproject --agent=gemini --auto
```

## Common Flags (all ntm commands)
```bash
--json           # Machine-readable JSON output
-v, --verbose    # Show detailed scoring/decision logs
-q, --quiet      # Suppress non-essential output
--timeout=30s    # Timeout for external calls (bv, br, Agent Mail)
--dry-run        # Alias for no --auto (preview mode)
--no-color       # Disable colored output
```

## Prompt Template System
Built-in templates:
- **impl**: "Work on bead {BEAD_ID}: {TITLE}. Check dependencies first with br dep tree {BEAD_ID}."
- **review**: "Review and verify bead {BEAD_ID}: {TITLE}. Run tests if applicable."
- **custom**: User provides template file

Variables available: {BEAD_ID}, {TITLE}, {DESCRIPTION}, {PRIORITY}, {TAGS}, {BLOCKED_BY}

## Agent Capability Matrix (for quality strategy)
```go
var AgentCapabilities = map[string]map[string]float64{
    "claude": {
        "refactoring": 0.95, "architecture": 0.95, "analysis": 0.90,
        "documentation": 0.85, "bug_fix": 0.85, "feature": 0.85,
        "testing": 0.75,
    },
    "codex": {
        "feature": 0.90, "bug_fix": 0.90, "testing": 0.85,
        "refactoring": 0.75, "documentation": 0.70,
    },
    "gemini": {
        "documentation": 0.90, "analysis": 0.85, "review": 0.90,
        "feature": 0.80, "testing": 0.80,
    },
}
```

## Algorithm
1. Get idle agents (context < 80%, no active assignment)
2. Get actionable beads from BV (not blocked)
3. Apply strategy scoring:
   - balanced: score = 1.0 / (current_assignments + 1)
   - speed: score = 1.0 - (context_usage / 100)
   - quality: score = capability_matrix[agent_type][bead_type]
   - dependency: score = unblocks_count * 0.5 + pagerank * 0.5
   - round-robin: deterministic index assignment
4. Generate (bead, agent) pairs sorted by score
5. If --auto: Send prompts, update assignment store
6. Emit JSON output

## Output Format
```json
{
  "strategy": "balanced",
  "assigned": [
    {
      "bead_id": "bd-abc1",
      "bead_title": "Implement feature X",
      "pane": 2,
      "agent_type": "claude",
      "agent_name": "GreenCastle",
      "score": 0.85,
      "prompt_sent": true
    }
  ],
  "skipped": [
    {
      "bead_id": "bd-def2",
      "reason": "blocked_by bd-abc1"
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total_beads": 10,
    "assigned": 4,
    "skipped": 6,
    "idle_agents": 4
  }
}
```

## Error Handling
- No idle agents: Return with "no_idle_agents" in output, suggest wait or spawn more
- No actionable beads: Return with "no_ready_beads" in output
- BV unavailable: Fall back to br ready list, warn user
- Send failure: Mark as failed in assignment store, include in output
